title.value: Stop Overthinking: A Survey on Efficient Reasoning for Large Language Models
title.evidence: Stop Overthinking: A Survey on Efficient Reasoning for Large Language Models

background.value: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as exceptionally powerful AI tools, demonstrating advanced capabilities in natural language understanding and complex reasoning.
background.evidence: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as exceptionally powerful AI tools, demonstrating advanced capabilities in natural language understanding and complex reasoning.

problem_statement.value: Efficient reasoning, particularly the reduction of reasoning length, offers significant benefits in such regards, providing direct cost reduction and improved feasibility for real-world deployments.
problem_statement.evidence: Efficient reasoning, particularly the reduction of reasoning length, offers significant benefits in such regards, providing direct cost reduction and improved feasibility for real-world deployments.

description.value: Model-based efficient reasoning focuses on fine-tuning LLMs to improve their intrinsic ability to reason concisely and efficiently.
description.evidence: From the model perspective, these works focus on fine-tuning LLMs to improve their intrinsic ability to reason concisely and efficiently.

examples.value: Reinforcement Learning with Length Reward Design, Supervised Fine-Tuning with Variable-Length CoT Data.
examples.evidence: Reinforcement Learning with Length Reward Design, Supervised Fine-Tuning with Variable-Length CoT Data.

description.value: Output-based efficient reasoning focuses on modifying the output paradigm to enhance the ability of LLMs to reason concisely and efficiently.
description.evidence: From the perspective of reasoning steps in the output, these works focus on modifying the output paradigm to enhance the ability of LLMs to reason concisely and efficiently.

examples.value: Compressing Reasoning Steps into Fewer Latent Representations, Dynamic Reasoning Paradigm during Inference.
examples.evidence: Compressing Reasoning Steps into Fewer Latent Representations, Dynamic Reasoning Paradigm during Inference.

description.value: Prompt-guided efficient reasoning explicitly instructs LLMs to generate fewer reasoning steps, can be a straightforward and highly effective method for improving the efficiency of reasoning models.
description.evidence: Prompt-guided efficient reasoning explicitly instructs LLMs to generate fewer reasoning steps, can be a straightforward and highly effective method for improving the efficiency of reasoning models.

performance_metrics.value: Models tend to perform better with extended reasoning steps, suggesting the CoT length is more crucial than accuracy for effective problem-solving.
performance_metrics.evidence: Models tend to perform better with extended reasoning steps, suggesting the CoT length is more crucial than accuracy for effective problem-solving.

results_summary.value: Efficient reasoning approaches offer significant practical benefits across various domains: reducing computational costs in healthcare diagnostics, enhancing real-time decision-making and safety in autonomous driving.
results_summary.evidence: Efficient reasoning approaches offer significant practical benefits across various domains: reducing computational costs in healthcare diagnostics, enhancing real-time decision-making and safety in autonomous driving.

conclusions.value: This paper provides the first structured survey of efficient reasoning in LLMs, categorizing existing approaches into three areas: model-based, reasoning output-based, and input prompts-based methods.
conclusions.evidence: This paper provides the first structured survey of efficient reasoning in LLMs, categorizing existing approaches into three areas: model-based, reasoning output-based, and input prompts-based methods.
